factions.name: The Achaean Host
factions.description: The coalition of Greek city-states besieging Troy. One hundred thousand men under [[Agamemnon]]'s nominal command, held together by oaths and ambition. Their unity is their weakness: the kings quarrel, the soldiers grow weary, and without [[Achilles]] they bleed against the Trojan walls.

log.note: Nine days of plague devastate the camp; the army demands answers. The seer Calchas reveals the cause: [[Agamemnon]]'s insult to Chryses.
[[The City of Troy|The Trojans]] breach the great wall of the Greek camp; [[Hector]] hurls a boulder through the gate and leads the charge toward the ships.
Consequence of The City of Troy (rival): Trojans fight on the Achaean beach for the first time in ten years.
Word of [[Patroclus]]'s death spreads through the Achaean camp; [[Achilles]]' cry of grief shakes the battlefield. Everyone knows what comes next.

factions.name: The City of Troy
factions.description: [[Priam]]'s city — rich, walled, and ten years under siege. The Trojans fight not for glory but for survival. Their greatest strength is [[Hector]]; their original wound is [[Paris]]. Troy is beloved of [[Apollo]] and Aphrodite, which keeps it standing long past reason.

log.note: Consequence of The Achaean Host (rival): Trojans breach the camp wall — Hector leads the charge to the ships.
For the first time in ten years [[The City of Troy|Trojans]] fight on the beach beside Achaean ships; the tide of the war shifts.
[[Hector]] is brought home. [[Andromache]], Hecuba, and [[Helen]] lead the lament. The poem ends here — not at Troy's fall, but at a funeral. That is Homer's answer to the question of what war is.

factions.name: The Divine Council
factions.description: The Olympian gods, who treat the Trojan War as their own theater. Zeus holds the scales of fate; Hera and [[Athena]] back the Achaeans; [[Apollo]] and Aphrodite shield Troy. Their interventions decide what human valor alone cannot. Hidden to mortal eyes.

log.note: Consequence of The Achaean Host (ally): Trojans breach the camp wall — Hector leads the charge to the ships.
Consequence of The City of Troy (ally): Trojans fight on the Achaean beach for the first time in ten years.
